name: Is there a deadline to file a claim for breathing problems that started years ago?

mainEntity.name: Is there a deadline to file a claim for breathing problems that started years ago?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, deadlines vary by state; Utah generally requires a claim within one year of discovering the illness is work-related, while Idaho has similar one-year constraints from manifestation. Because respiratory issues develop slowly, the clock usually starts at the moment of medical diagnosis.
acceptedAnswer.description: In Utah, the statute of limitations for workers\' compensation is generally one year from the date of injury or the date you should have known the illness was work-related, though some occupational disease claims have longer windows up to several years. In Idaho, you generally have one year from the date of manifestation to file a formal claim with the Commission. Respiratory illnesses are tricky because they often develop slowly over years of "micro-exposures." The clock usually starts when a doctor first tells you, "Your lung issues are caused by your job." Waiting even a few weeks after that diagnosis can jeopardize your entire claim. If you think you\'ve missed a deadline, an attorney can sometimes identify exceptions based on when the "discovery" occurred.
